title: localization — subprocessors
description: Machine-translation providers process source strings + return translated strings. Only engaged when tenant_locale.auto_translate=true. See compliance.json for GDPR Art. 28 obligations.

subprocessors.name: OpenAI
subprocessors.purpose: Context-aware translation via chat completion. Best for UI copy + domain-specific terminology.
data_processed: source strings passed to translation prompt
subprocessors.location: US default; EU processing via zero-data-retention EU deployment (config('localization.openai.region'))

subprocessors.name: Google Cloud Translation
subprocessors.purpose: Broad language coverage + cheapest at scale.
subprocessors.location: US / EU / APAC (customer-selected via project region)

subprocessors.name: DeepL
subprocessors.purpose: Highest quality on European languages. EU-native provider.
subprocessors.location: EU (Germany); does not store request payloads (DeepL Pro terms)

subprocessors.name: AWS Translate
subprocessors.purpose: AWS-native. Good when the app is on AWS.

notes: The 'null' driver is a no-op used in tests and when auto-translate is off. Not a subprocessor.
Tenant-level opt-in means auto_translate=false is the default; tenants must explicitly consent by flipping the toggle in the settings UI.
